titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Brixianus
VL10
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Jo Lc Mc

condition.p: Some pages very badly preserved, with parchment eroded by ink and preserved by gauze; others in excellent condition.

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

listBibl.bibl: In some places these are clearer than the manuscript in its current state.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010. The manuscript was consulted in person by Hugh Houghton on 3.12.2010, and most unclear readings were resolved.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 152r-240v. The first part of the manuscript (ff. 1-38), also on purple parchment, was added later. It includes canon tables with the opening text of each Ammonian Section. The text of the manuscript is also given in full in J. Wordsworth and H.J. White (edd.), Novum Testamentum Domini Nostri Iesu Christi Secundum Editionem Sancti Hieronymi. Pars Prior: Evangelia. Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1889-1898.The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation, abbreviations and digraphs. Ammonian Section numbers are usually included at the beginning of each verse to which they pertain. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton. The editorial notes still need reincorporating.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Correction to John 4:39, and change to verse division in 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

seg.note: The quire signature QXIIII is visible at the bottom of the previous page (otherwise blank): John therefore begins at the beginning of Quire 15

ab.note: Later repair to the parchment makes it impossible to determine the original reading.
supplied from WW
The first hand initially omitted 'est'; the end of the line was then rewritten with 'monium' projecting into the margin.
The last line and three preceding letters have been added later, possibly by the first hand.
This page has been conserved by a superimposed gauze, which makes reading the text very difficult.
obscured by parchment repair
Much of this page has been obscured by repair to the parchment; the text has been taken from Wordsworth White.

From the beginning of 1:46 to the end of the page, the text has been rewritten, but is obscured by parchment repair, and is based on Wordsworth White (with approximate lineation). Two capital 'E's are visible in the margin from the first hand.
Wordsworth-White have 'excitabo', but 'excitauis' is clear from the original.
No quire signature is visible at the bottom of this page (it should be the end of Quire XU).
trimmed, but letters visible
WW credit
WW erat hora
Wordsworth-White read 'galilaeam', but no superline is visible.
Quire signature QXUI is visible at the bottom of the page.
WW quaecunque
Wordsworth-White read 'figuram', but no superline is visible.
'est' is a later addition, in smaller script.
The number of the 'lec' is missing; it should be 10.
Quire signature QXUII is visible at the bottom of the page.
The last four letters of this line project into the right margin: they may have originally been omitted through haplography with 'et' on the next line, but they seem to be first hand.
There is a slight possibility that the word 'misit' may have been erased with a dry point, but if so, it was much less effective than later erasures.
The number of the 'lec' is missing; it should be 13.
The words 's(an)c(tu)s in eis' may have been erased.
Quire signature QXUIII is visible at the bottom of the page.
WW et
There is a small cross above the 'm' of 'peccatum'.
WW proterea
sic
No quire signature is visible at the bottom of the page.
'In' projects into the left margin and appears to be a later addition.
WW crediditis
Wordsworth-White read nouissimo, but nouissima is clear.
This page only has 19 rather than the usual 20 lines. Quire signature QXX is visible at the bottom of the page.
'in cordibus uestris' has been scratched out with a dry point.
'tauit' is added in smaller letters in the margin, perhaps by a different hand.
The four lines beginning 'quia', 'gente', 'te' and 'erant' have a different ink consistency, and may have been written above an erasure.
WW est
WW tonitruum
The first hand seems to have written 'mundum' twice (as 'mundumundum') followed by 'ueni', then erased 'undum ueni' and re-written 'ueni' earlier in the line.
The top of the quire signature is visible at the bottom of the page, but the rest has been cut off.
The word 'cognoscetis' has been added in smaller letters on the right.
erased by heavy scratching
The quire number QXXII is in the bottom margin.
WW quia
WW does not note correction
The erased letters are impossible to make out.
corrected on basis of WW and spacing
corrected on basis of WW and spacing
restored from WW based on spacing
The quire number QXXIII is in the bottom margin.
The dot is missing from the expected m-overline.
The expected Ammonian Section at the beginning of 19:7 is 'cxci', with 'cxc' at 'ego enim' in the previous verse.
Wordsworth White omit 'ei'.
transcription had crucifigerent
The word 'suum' has been rubbed: this may be an attempt to erase it, but it is not as definite as the scratched-out words elsewhere.
This 'lec' mark is added in a different ink and by a different hand to those earlier in the manuscript.
There is no obvious reason for the spaces between the letters h-o-c.
The rest of the page is blank. Fol 241r and 241v are blank apart from the quire signature in the bottom margin of 241v. The manuscript continues with Luke on 242r.
